full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Designing a failure scenario that exposes a fundamental limitation of arc-cosine kernels
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Help me construct a scenario where the arc-cosine kernel fails. I want to demonstrate the dynamic range explosion when using n=2 at ALL layers of the multilayer recursion (violating the norm-preservation assumption from Task 1.2). Provide code comparing correct (n=2 at layer 1, n=1 at rest) vs broken (n=2 everywhere) at depths 1-4, plus badly-scaled data.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Provided the broken multilayer kernel implementation that uses uniform degree at all layers, the comparison framework across depths, and the badly-scaled data construction.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: I chose the specific failure scenario myself (n=2 at all layers + bad scaling) based on the norm-preservation assumption I identified in Task 1.2. Added the kernel matrix condition number logging which the LLM did not include. Also added the histogram visualization of kernel value distributions which I designed to show the dynamic range explosion visually.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified the dynamic range analysis by manually computing k_2(x,x) for a sample vector and checking it grows as ||x||^4 per layer. Confirmed the condition numbers increase with depth as predicted.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Writing the failure explanation paragraph and suggested fix
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Write a 5-7 sentence explanation of why the n=2-at-all-layers approach fails, connecting to Assumption 1 from Task 1.2 (norm preservation). Include one sentence suggesting layer-wise kernel normalization as a fix.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Drafted an explanation connecting the exponential magnitude growth to the norm-preservation assumption and suggesting the normalization fix.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified the magnitude growth formula ||x||^(2*2^l) matches the theoretical prediction from Eq. 3 and the recursive formula Eq. 12. Confirmed the normalization fix is mathematically sound (dividing by geometric mean of diagonals).

top_5_prompts.prompt: Construct a failure scenario demonstrating dynamic range explosion with n=2 at all layers, violating norm-preservation.
top_5_prompts.why_important: This was the key prompt that set up the entire failure mode demonstration — the core of Task 3.2.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Explain why the n=2-at-all-layers approach fails, connecting to Assumption 1 from Task 1.2, with a suggested fix.
top_5_prompts.why_important: Produced the analytical explanation linking the failure to the specific assumption, as required by guidelines.

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for Task 3.2 of Part B.
